messages.content: What is the latest version of Python?

{"query": "latest version of python", "top_k": [{"url": "https://devguide.python.org/versions/", "title": "Status of Python versions - Python Developer's Guide", "content": "The main branch is currently the future Python 3.15, and is the only branch that accepts new features. The latest release for each Python version can be found", "score": 0.99371004, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.python.org/doc/versions/", "title": "Python documentation by version", "content": "Python 3.11.15, released on 3 March 2026 \u00b7 Python 3.11.14, released on 9 October 2025 \u00b7 Python 3.11.13, released on 3 June 2025 \u00b7 Python 3.11.12, released on 8", "score": 0.9845754, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/python/download-and-install-python-3-latest-version/", "title": "Download and Install Python 3 Latest Version - GeeksforGeeks", "content": "Open your browser and visit the Python download page: python.org/downloads. Select the latest Python 3 version, such as Python 3.13.1 (", "score": 0.9843364, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.python.org/", "title": "Welcome to Python.org", "content": "Download. Python source code and installers are available for download for all versions! Latest: Python 3.14.3. Docs.", "score": 0.98087597, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.python.org/getit/windows/", "title": "Python Releases for Windows", "content": "Stable Releases ; Python install manager 26.0 - Feb. 23, 2026 ; Python 3.13.12 - Feb. 3, 2026. Note that Python 3.13.12 cannot be used on Windows 7 or earlier.", "score": 0.97046465, "raw_content": null}]}

{"query": "Python 3.15 release date", "top_k": [{"url": "https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/", "title": "PEP 790 \u2013 Python 3.15 Release Schedule", "content": "# PEP 790 \u2013 Python 3.15 Release Schedule | peps.python.org. *   [PEP Index](https://peps.python.org/pep-0000/) \u00bb. Toggle light / dark / auto colour theme PEP 790 \u2013 Python 3.15 Release Schedule. # PEP 790 \u2013 Python 3.15 Release Schedule. Author:Hugo van Kemenade Status:Active Type:Informational Topic:[Release](https://peps.python.org/topic/release/)Created:26-Apr-2025 Python-Version:3.15. *   [Abstract](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#abstract). *   [Release manager and crew](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#release-manager-and-crew). *   [Release schedule](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#release-schedule). *   [3.15.0 schedule](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#schedule). *   [3.15 lifespan](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#lifespan). *   [Copyright](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#copyright). ## [Abstract](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#abstract). This document describes the development and release schedule for Python 3.15. ## [Release manager and crew](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#release-manager-and-crew). ## [Release schedule](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#release-schedule). ### [3.15.0 schedule](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#schedule). The dates below use a 17-month development period that results in a 12-month release cadence between feature versions, as defined by [PEP 602](https://peps.python.org/pep-0602/ \"PEP 602 \u2013 Annual Release Cycle for Python\"). *   3.15.0 alpha 1: Tuesday, 2025-10-14. *   3.15.0 beta 2: Tuesday, 2026-06-02. *   3.15.0 beta 3: Tuesday, 2026-06-23. ### [3.15 lifespan](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#lifespan). ## [Copyright](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#copyright). *   [Abstract](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#abstract). *   [Release manager and crew](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#release-manager-and-crew). *   [Release schedule](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#release-schedule). *   [3.15.0 schedule](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#schedule). *   [3.15 lifespan](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#lifespan). *   [Copyright](https://peps.python.org/pep-0790/#copyright). [Page Source (GitHub)](https://github.com/python/peps/blob/main/peps/pep-0790.rst?plain=1).", "score": 0.926683, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.reddit.com/r/Python/comments/1kbvpr4/pep_790_python_315_release_schedule/", "title": "PEP 790 \u2013 Python 3.15 Release Schedule", "content": "* 3.15.0 alpha 1: Tuesday, 2025-10-14. * 3.15.0 alpha 2: Tuesday, 2025-11-18. * 3.15.0 alpha 3: Tuesday, 2025-12-16. * 3.15.0 alpha 4: Tuesday, 2026-01-13. * 3.15.0 alpha 5: Tuesday, 2026-02-10. * 3.15.0 alpha 6: Tuesday, 2026-03-10. * 3.15.0 alpha 7: Tuesday, 2026-04-07. * 3.15.0 beta 2: Tuesday, 2026-05-26. * 3.15.0 beta 3: Tuesday, 2026-06-16. * 3.15.0 beta 4: Tuesday, 2026-07-14. * 3.15.0 candidate 1: Tuesday, 2026-07-28. * 3.15.0 candidate 2: Tuesday, 2026-09-01. * Python 3.15 will receive bugfix updates approximately every second month for two years. * Around the time of the release of 3.18.0 final, the final 3.15 bugfix update will be released. * After that, it is expected that security updates (source only) will be released for the next three years, until five years after the release of 3.15.0 final, so until approximately October 2031. ## Top Posts. * reReddit: Top posts of May 1, 2025. reReddit: Top posts of May 2025. reReddit: Top posts of 2025.", "score": 0.9020676, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://gdevops.frama.io/python/versions/3.15.0/3.15.0.html", "title": "Python 3.15.0 (2026-10-01)", "content": "# **Python 3.15.0** (2026-10-01) \u00b6. (Python 3.15 Release Schedule). ## Summary \u00b6. ## New features \u00b6. ## Removals \u00b6. ## lifespan \u00b6. Python 3.15 will receive bugfix updates approximately every second. Around the time of the release of 3.18.0 final, the final 3.15 bugfix. After that, it is expected that security updates (source only) will. be released for the next three years, until five years after the. release of 3.15.0 final, so until approximately October 2031.", "score": 0.8967681, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-3150a2/", "title": "Python Release Python 3.15.0a2", "content": "The next pre-release of Python 3.15 will be 3.15.0a3, currently scheduled for 2025-12-16.
